last_completed_item: Generated and executable-validator-validated the human-approved WP-001 through WP-012 amended five-weapon batch with numeric-only empowerment, exclusive package ownership, generated-registry safety, temporary presentation, formal evidence and an explicit human quality gate; no game implementation was performed.
current_item: Human review of draft PR #9 for the validated stage1-weapons batch on ai/task-split-shooter-mover-v1-continuation-5.
next_action: Review and merge stage1-weapons draft PR #9. After it merges, start a fresh Task Splitter context from current main, present EN-001 through EN-013 titles, owner lanes and exact dependencies for stage1-enemies-route, and stop. Do not generate the enemy/route batch before explicit proposal approval.
resume_instruction: Read assembly/context/NEW_CHAT_RESUME.md first and follow it exactly.
blockers: Ten planned agent batches remain to be proposed, approved and generated one at a time.

Foundation, Contract Steward and Evidence Harness estimates total 10.9 focused lead days against the accepted S1.0 cap of 5 days; Dispatch requires human re-estimation, scope sequencing or an approved milestone-cap amendment. Evidence-critical contracts, accessibility, diagnostics, reliability and performance may not be cut to hide the overrun.
Movement-thruster estimates 6.15 focused lead days against the accepted S1.1 cap of 8 days, leaving 1.85 days for human review and bounded evidence-led iteration; Dispatch must preserve that reserve.

Combat-four-mount plus stage1-weapons consume 9.10 focused lead days of the accepted S1.2 ten-day cap, leaving only 0.90 day for review reserve and bounded approved follow-up; Dispatch may not add generalized tooling, mature balance depth or extra weapon breadth.
The three remaining Stage 2 weapon identities are deliberately deferred until Stage 1 evidence and require a later planning amendment before Stage 2 combat-content generation or dispatch.
The canonical task backlog and collaboration-state finalization remain blocked until all indexed batches validate.
Implementation and Dispatch remain blocked until the completed task-split pull request is explicitly approved and merged.

verification_boundary.unselected_question: D-234 was not accepted; the merged planning package defines the prototype-debt register and exit policy.

all MT tasks are size S or M and depend only on existing UF/CS/EH tasks or earlier MT tasks

MT-007 exclusively owns ShooterMoverMovement.inputactions; MT-012 reads but does not edit the evidence-owned benchmark scene

CB-003 introduces no passive power regeneration; CB-008 depends on MT-007 lifecycle conventions; CB-010 projects HUD state from plain C# Application code; CB-011 requires recorded human playable review

the amended roster is exactly Blaster Machine Gun, Shotgun, Rocket Launcher, Arc Gun and Ricochet Gun; empowerment is numeric-only, Arc is capped at three additional targets and Ricochet at two wall bounces

the six generated batches contain 68 tasks with an acyclic topologically ordered dependency graph and 227 non-overlapping owned path claims
the weapon batch records 4.60 focused lead days; combined S1.2 direct spend is 9.10 of 10 days and preserves 0.90 day of review reserve
no Unity game implementation, canonical backlog, collaboration assignment, weapon content implementation, enemy content, mission, reward or persistence task batch was created
